summary: Plan-only classification for one hydrated PR inventory shard. PR #89816 is a focused docs-only mitigation for issue #89791, but merge recommendation is blocked because the hydrated preflight lacks required Codex /review merge proof and the latest ClawSweeper review explicitly leaves maintainer acceptance of operator-facing launchd risk before merge.

actions.reason: Merge proof is incomplete and maintainer acceptance is still required for the documented launchd workaround risk, but the PR is narrow, credited contributor work and can be repaired/finalized without broad code changes.
#89791 is the related canonical tracking issue for remaining runtime and install/doctor work; it should stay open and should not be closed by this docs-only PR plan.

needs_human: Maintainer acceptance is required before merging #89816 because the docs publish a manual launchd workaround that can disable the node LaunchAgent on hosts relying on remote node features, even though the PR added an explicit skip warning.
A complete merge recommendation for #89816 still needs Codex /review merge-preflight proof and addressed findings; the hydrated artifact does not include that proof.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair/finalize contributor PR #89816 in place if maintainers accept the documented operator-facing launchd risk. The branch is narrow, maintainer-editable, docs-only, and already credits the source issue; the remaining work is merge-preflight completion rather than a replacement implementation.
repair_candidate.pr_title: docs(gateway): add launchd supervisor loop troubleshooting
repair_candidate.pr_body: Finalize contributor PR #89816 by Pick-cat after maintainer acceptance of the launchd runbook risk and a clean Codex /review. This remains docs-only mitigation for #89791 and must not auto-close #89791; runtime wrapper generation and doctor/install hardening stay tracked there.
credit_notes: Preserve Pick-cat attribution if maintainers repair or finalize the branch.
Related issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89791 remains open for runtime wrapper and doctor/install hardening follow-up.
